alerts.title: Iran-Israel missile exchanges escalate; both sides signal operational pause
alerts.summary: Iran and Israel exchanged multiple waves of ballistic missiles and airstrikes overnight (June 7-8). Iran launched 12+ missiles from Tabriz and Isfahan; Israel conducted ~20 retaliatory strikes across Tehran, Tabriz, Isfahan, Qeredagh, and Kermanshah. Both sides announced operational halts per Khatam Al-Anbiyaa Central Command and Israeli media, but ceasefire fragility remains acute. This continues the kinetic direct warfare phase first breached in previous alert cycle.

alerts.title: Iran nuclear breakout timeline remains 1-2 weeks at 60% U-235 enrichment
alerts.summary: IAEA and US intelligence confirm Iranian uranium enrichment unchanged at 60% U-235 purity with breakout window estimated at 1-2 weeks, per global_metrics nuclear headlines. US draft IAEA resolution demands Iran disclose fate of previously bombed nuclear sites and current uranium stocks. Simultaneous kinetic escalation and unchanged accelerated nuclear timeline present compounding strategic crisis.

alerts.title: Houthis renew Red Sea blockade declaration against Israeli shipping
alerts.summary: Iran-backed Houthis issued renewed threats to block Israeli vessels from Red Sea transit, per multiple incident reports (Reuters, Jerusalem Post, Al-Monitor). Declaration follows Iran-Israel escalation and signals proxy force coordination. Four Houthi-attributed naval incident reports clustered in same reporting cycle indicate elevated operational posture.

alerts.title: Trump diverges from Netanyahu over Iran retaliation; doctrine inconsistency exposed
alerts.summary: Rybar Telegram analysis (~146K views) reports Trump told Netanyahu 'war will soon end' and called for de-escalation, yet Israel proceeded with ~20 strikes on Iranian targets overnight. Trump also stated Netanyahu 'will have no choice but to accept a deal with Iran,' but Israeli operations continued unabated. Signal of fractured US-Israel strategic alignment on escalation control.

alerts.title: US negative sentiment holds at 51% as Trump dominance persists in news cycle
alerts.summary: US sentiment analysis shows negative sentiment at 51% (vs 42% positive). Word frequency analysis shows 'Trump' leading all terms (63 mentions) alongside 'Iran' (18) and 'house/white' (12/10), reflecting continued focus on Trump statements regarding Iran conflict. Sentiment remains elevated but stable from prior cycle, indicating public anxiety persists without dramatic swing.

analysis_note: Direct Iran-Israel kinetic exchange continues with both sides declaring operational pauses; nuclear breakout timeline unchanged at 1-2 weeks; Houthi proxy activation compounds theater escalation; US-Israel alignment fractures over escalation doctrine.
